instruction:
Most credit cards charge interest based on the average daily balance per billing cycle. In this case each balance within the billing cycle is weighted by the number of days it exists. Suppose your credit card has a 30 day billing cycle and the balances over these 30 days are given in the table below.
$\begin{array}{cccc}\hline Days & Transaction & balance & # days \\ \hline 1-8 & remaining balance & \$1500 & 8 \\ \hline 9-14 & \$400 purchase & \$1900 & 6 \\ \hline 15-20 & \$400 purchase & \$2300 & 6 \\ \hline 21-30 & \$1400 payment & \$900 & 10 \\ \hline \end{array}$ 10
(a) Calculate the average daily balance for these 30 days. [ANS]
(b) What would have been your average daily balance if you paid \$1400 on day 17 instead of \$1400 on day 21? [ANS]
